memory.summary: I'm stressed about that project, but I can't bring myself to start yet. Relaxing with friends feels like the only way to handle this.
Teammates know I'm just chilling in the dorm, not actually working. That's fine; we can renegotiate the deadline later if things fall apart.
Teammates caught me stalling again, but they're okay with it since we can renegotiate later. Honestly, working feels like too much right now.
I told the cohort I'm crashing and they accepted it. My trust with them is high, so I'll just renegotiate later without guilt.
Teammates are chill about my stalling, but I'm realizing the afternoon is slipping away and the project still hasn't started.
I just messaged the cohort to regroup, and they're already reacting. This feels like a solid move since I can't trust myself to keep stalling.
I finally stopped stalling and messaged the cohort to regroup. Good, because I can't trust myself to keep delaying.
I just confirmed the new timeline with my cohort, cutting off any more stalling. They're already reacting, which is exactly what I needed.
They're already reacting to my timeline update. Solid move since I can't trust myself to keep stalling anyway.
I finally stopped stalling and locked in the new timeline with my cohort. They're already reacting, which proves I can't trust myself to keep delaying anyway.
I just pivoted to save our group dynamic, and they're already reacting. Finally stopped stalling.
Locked the timeline with my cohort to protect our dynamic; they're already reacting, so I finally stopped stalling.

desires: Keep the cohort_3 group dynamic intact and avoid any perception of being unreliable now that I've finally locked in a timeline.
Secure an instant win on that trivial chore before bed to feel like I'm actually moving forward, even if it's just for tonight.
Avoid having to explain or renegotiate the project timeline again by starting the Term Project immediately.

goals.description: Send the follow-up text to cohort_3 immediately, explicitly framing the timeline shift as a 'necessary pivot' to cement trust and avoid any perception of unreliability.
goals.rationale: I just hit them with the hard truth ('Frida'), but now I need to seal the deal before they start making excuses. My relationships are worth more than this task; if I waffle now, they'll think I'm unreliable and ruin the group dynamic. I'm not playing games; I'm locking in a win right now so panic doesn't set in. The hesitation earlier was weakness, and I won't let it happen again.

goals.description: Complete the trivial easy chore immediately after sending the text to get an instant dopamine hit and clear mental clutter.
goals.rationale: I've been planning to sleep first to fix my stress, but since the project isn't actually hard work yet, I can wait until the chat is settled. However, if I let this pile up, I'll feel like a bad person tomorrow. The goal is just to clear that mental clutter so I can rest properly tonight without guilt. It's a small win, but it keeps my energy up for the big term project later.

agent.last_reasoning: Relationships are worth more than the trivial chore. I need to explicitly close this loop immediately to cement trust and prevent any perception of unreliability. If I wait, they'll make excuses. I'm locking in a win right now before panic sets in.

goals.description: Rejoin the group chat immediately with a casual update to avoid looking like a slacker.
goals.rationale: My value system weighs relationships (0.5) way higher than task outcomes. If I don't check in now, my social standing drops and it creates awkwardness with the cohort_3 group. Better to drop the big project for three hours than risk feeling excluded.

goals.description: Do the trivial easy chore right after chatting just to feel useful without stressing about the term project.
goals.rationale: I need that instant win of 'usefulness' to balance out the stress, but only if I'm not doing hard work. Since the term project is due Thursday and I barely started, pushing this too early will just add pressure. I'll wait until after I relax with friends.

desires: Get back into the group chat before they ask if I'm okay so I don't look like a slacker.
Finish that tiny chore tonight just to feel useful without having to think about the big project.
Skip the hard work for three more hours and actually relax with friends instead of stressing out alone.

goals.description: Hit snooze and go to sleep. No more thinking about the project or chores.
goals.rationale: I have 2 hours of sleep debt and zero motivation to do anything hard before the deadline hits. My brain is fried, and doing nothing guarantees I won't crash tomorrow. The term project is due in 3 days; I can handle that stress if I rest first. Anything else is just burning energy I don't have.

desires: Not feel like I'm wasting my friends' time by dragging out the renegotiation talk.
Get some actual sleep before the deadline hits so I don't crash tomorrow.
